eventData.messageContent: Own the Node.js and TypeScript implementation plan, code structure, dependency choices, and concrete build guidance.
Own the Node.js and TypeScript implementation plan, code structure, dependency choices, and concrete build guidance.
Own Dockerization, runtime configuration, execution workflow, and operational handoff for running the solution reliably.
Own Dockerization, runtime configuration, execution workflow, and operational handoff for running the solution reliably.
Audit implementation completeness, TypeScript and container risks, and confirm the final workflow is reviewable and executable.
Audit implementation completeness, TypeScript and container risks, and confirm the final workflow is reviewable and executable.

Produce an execution playbook for running the Node.js/TypeScript solution in Docker: define container startup workflow, required runtime configuration inputs, rollout steps by environment, acceptance signals, support handoff checklist, and key operational risks or ownership gaps.

Produce an execution playbook for running the Node.js/TypeScript solution in Docker: define container startup workflow, required runtime configuration inputs, rollout steps by environment, acceptance signals, support handoff checklist, and key operational risks or ownership gaps.

Specify the tool and MCP integration contract needed for the Dockerized runtime workflow: list each connector purpose, required inputs/secrets/config, expected outputs, dependency notes, and fallback procedures when a tool or MCP server is unavailable.

Specify the tool and MCP integration contract needed for the Dockerized runtime workflow: list each connector purpose, required inputs/secrets/config, expected outputs, dependency notes, and fallback procedures when a tool or MCP server is unavailable.

Audit the proposed workflow for implementation completeness and reviewability. Check whether the Node.js workflow is fully specified end-to-end, TypeScript (.ts) handling is concrete, Docker usage is executable, and claimed steps are supported by evidence/examples rather than generic statements. Return: (1) confirmed complete elements, (2) missing or weakly specified elements, (3) unclear assumptions, and (4) exact revisions needed before QA sign-off.

Audit the proposed workflow for implementation completeness and reviewability. Check whether the Node.js workflow is fully specified end-to-end, TypeScript (.ts) handling is concrete, Docker usage is executable, and claimed steps are supported by evidence/examples rather than generic statements. Return: (1) confirmed complete elements, (2) missing or weakly specified elements, (3) unclear assumptions, and (4) exact revisions needed before QA sign-off.

Assess execution risk across TypeScript and Docker concerns. Identify dependency, build, runtime, environment, and adoption risks in the Node.js/TypeScript/Docker workflow; call out required tool inputs/connectors if any are implied; and provide fallback paths when tooling or environment assumptions fail. Return: (1) prioritized risks with impact, (2) evidence gaps, (3) mitigation actions, and (4) minimum conditions required for the workflow to be executable.

Identify dependency, build, runtime, environment, and adoption risks in the Node.js/TypeScript/Docker workflow; call out required tool inputs/connectors if any are implied; and provide fallback paths when tooling or environment assumptions fail. Return: (1) prioritized risks with impact, (2) evidence gaps, (3) mitigation actions, and (4) minimum conditions required for the workflow to be executable.
